figures.caption: Table 3: Rigid-body oracle results (600 samples, 3 runs per geometry)
Table 5: Complete invariant vector: oloid vs. cylinder across five physical domains
Table 4: Hertz oracle results: CDS vs. SDS (600 samples, 3 runs per geometry)

Figure 1: Left: CDS scores under the rigid-body oracle (log scale). The oloid (gold) scores 8.2×10−7; the cylinder (red) scores 4.75×10−5 (58× worse). Green bars show three asymmetric two-circle roller variants, all within 2.4× of the oloid. Right: Oloid construction from two generating circles C1 and C2 in perpendicular planes.
